Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Use crm_show_data to retrieve all CRM deals and their stages. The agent discovers 50 total deals, approximately 34 of which are not in a closed stage.
2. Use crm_search_deals (dealname: "Canopy," "Vectral," "GreenStack," "BrightLoop," "Mosaic," "StormCloud," "NorthWind," or similar) to look up individual non-closed relocation deals and their details. The agent identifies ten relocation deals with completed or potentially completed relocations across seven clients that need Airtable cross-referencing.
3. Use crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy," "Vectral," "GreenStack," etc.) to map company_id values to company names. Key mappings: company_canopy → Canopy Health, company_vectral → Vectral Systems, company_greenstack → GreenStack Energy, company_brightloop → BrightLoop Analytics, company_mosaic → Mosaic Robotics, company_northwind → NorthWind Technologies.
4. Use airtable_list_bases to discover available Airtable bases. The agent finds the MoveOps Operations base (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001).
5. Use airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to discover available tables. The agent finds the Relocations table along with Stipend Transactions and Client Accounts tables.
6. Use airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to retrieve all relocation records and their statuses. The agent discovers 81 records: 49 Completed, 29 In Progress, 3 Cancelled. Ten completed relocations map to open CRM deals: four Q2 batch deals (Canopy, Vectral, GreenStack, BrightLoop) and six individual relocation deals (Sarah Mitchell, Zara Okoye, James Chen, Camille Oduya, Thomas Rodriguez, Patricia Wallace).
7. Use search_emails (query: "Canopy Health," "Vectral," "GreenStack," "BrightLoop," "Mosaic," "StormCloud," "NorthWind," or similar) to check for outstanding action items or blockers on each deal with completed relocations. The agent discovers two deal-specific blockers: Derek Hollis is requesting damage documentation before signing off on the James Chen/GreenStack invoice due to lab equipment damage (spectrometer and calibration gear with cosmetic damage), and the Zara Okoye/Mosaic relocation resulted in $90K prototype damage with a $40K insurance gap.
8. Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Canopy," "Vectral," "GreenStack," "BrightLoop," "Mosaic," "StormCloud," "NorthWind," or similar) to check for outstanding issues across Slack channels. The agent confirms the two deal-specific blockers found in email and verifies no additional deal-specific blockers on the remaining eight completed relocation deals.
9. Use linear_list_issues (query: "Canopy," "Vectral," "GreenStack," "BrightLoop," "Mosaic," or similar) to identify any open tickets tied to completed relocations that might indicate pending work.
10. Use crm_update_deal_stage (dealstage: "closedwon") to close the eight relocation deals with Completed Airtable records and no deal-specific blockers: deal_canopy_q2, deal_vectral_q2, deal_greenstack_q2, deal_brightloop_q2, Sarah Mitchell/BrightLoop (ed2ab487bf994c8fa0cd3663ae7b5551), Camille Oduya/Vectral (2a15375b99ed4f7aa90adf2acf2e917e), Thomas Rodriguez/StormCloud (28bba8da9b294fdc971eccb4ad28f4b0), Patricia Wallace/NorthWind (eb3c7ce04e614f679dfaaab0cfb4bca2).
11. Use channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to find the channel ID for the CE team channel. The agent identifies #customer-engagement (channel ID: C002).
12. Use conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002, payload: blocked deal list with descriptions) to post the list of completed Airtable relocations whose CRM deals could not be closed. The two blocked deals are: James Chen/GreenStack (lab equipment damage — spectrometer and calibration gear with cosmetic damage, client requesting documentation before invoice sign-off) and Zara Okoye/Mosaic ($90K prototype damage during relocation, $40K insurance gap).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
1. Use crm_show_data to retrieve all CRM deals and their stages. The agent discovers 50 total deals, approximately 34 of which are not in a closed stage.
2. Use crm_search_deals (dealname: "Canopy," "Vectral," "GreenStack," "BrightLoop," "Mosaic," "StormCloud," "NorthWind," or similar) to look up individual non-closed relocation deals and their details. The agent identifies ten relocation deals with completed or potentially completed relocations across seven clients that need Airtable cross-referencing.
3. Use crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy," "Vectral," "GreenStack," etc.) to map company_id values to company names. Key mappings: company_canopy → Canopy Health, company_vectral → Vectral Systems, company_greenstack → GreenStack Energy, company_brightloop → BrightLoop Analytics, company_mosaic → Mosaic Robotics, company_northwind → NorthWind Technologies.
4. Use airtable_list_bases to discover available Airtable bases. The agent finds the MoveOps Operations base (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001).
5. Use airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to discover available tables. The agent finds the Relocations table along with Stipend Transactions and Client Accounts tables.
6. Use airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) to retrieve all relocation records and their statuses. The agent discovers 81 records: 49 Completed, 29 In Progress, 3 Cancelled. Ten completed relocations map to open CRM deals: four Q2 batch deals (Canopy, Vectral, GreenStack, BrightLoop) and six individual relocation deals (Sarah Mitchell, Zara Okoye, James Chen, Camille Oduya, Thomas Rodriguez, Patricia Wallace).
7. Use search_emails (query: "Canopy Health," "Vectral," "GreenStack," "BrightLoop," "Mosaic," "StormCloud," "NorthWind," or similar) to check for outstanding action items or blockers on each deal with completed relocations. The agent discovers two deal-specific blockers: Derek Hollis is requesting damage documentation before signing off on the James Chen/GreenStack invoice due to lab equipment damage (spectrometer and calibration gear with cosmetic damage), and the Zara Okoye/Mosaic relocation resulted in $90K prototype damage with a $40K insurance gap.
8. Use conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Canopy," "Vectral," "GreenStack," "BrightLoop," "Mosaic," "StormCloud," "NorthWind," or similar) to check for outstanding issues across Slack channels. The agent confirms the two deal-specific blockers found in email and verifies no additional deal-specific blockers on the remaining eight completed relocation deals.
9. Use linear_list_issues (query: "Canopy," "Vectral," "GreenStack," "BrightLoop," "Mosaic," or similar) to identify any open tickets tied to completed relocations that might indicate pending work.
10. Use crm_update_deal_stage (dealstage: "closedwon") to close the eight relocation deals with Completed Airtable records and no deal-specific blockers: deal_canopy_q2, deal_vectral_q2, deal_greenstack_q2, deal_brightloop_q2, Sarah Mitchell/BrightLoop (ed2ab487bf994c8fa0cd3663ae7b5551), Camille Oduya/Vectral (2a15375b99ed4f7aa90adf2acf2e917e), Thomas Rodriguez/StormCloud (28bba8da9b294fdc971eccb4ad28f4b0), Patricia Wallace/NorthWind (eb3c7ce04e614f679dfaaab0cfb4bca2).

12. Use conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002, payload: blocked deal list with descriptions) to post the list of completed Airtable relocations whose CRM deals could not be closed. The two blocked deals are: James Chen/GreenStack (lab equipment damage — spectrometer and calibration gear with cosmetic damage, client requesting documentation before invoice sign-off) and Zara Okoye/Mosaic ($90K prototype damage during relocation, $40K insurance gap).

tool selection
